title: Packet hydration auto-emits PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX rows from HBR registry
goal: Extend the existing packet hydration tooling so that on every packet hydration pass it reads the HBR registry (via MT-001 loader), evaluates each rule's applicability against the packet's metadata + touched-path declaration, and writes one PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX row per Applicable rule (status=PENDING). NotApplicable rules emit zero rows and carry a captured reason in a parallel ledger so validators do not need to re-evaluate.
expected_diff_shape: New Node module .GOV/roles_shared/scripts/hbr-matrix-hydrate.mjs invoked by existing packet hydration pipeline; integration test fixture confirming the right rows are emitted for a sample packet.
unblock_conditions: MT-001 registry loader and applicability evaluator merged.
implementation_notes: Per spec §5.6 and the enforcement.mechanism field of HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json (`PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX_EXTENSION`), this MT closes the loop from registry to packet acceptance matrix. The hydrator: (1) reads the packet.json (must accept a CLI arg `--packet <path>`), (2) extracts touched-path declarations (scope.allowed_paths globs), (3) extracts declared tags (a new optional `hbr.tags_declared` field on packet.json; absence is fine — evaluator falls back to path-glob-based tag inference), (4) extracts `hbr.not_applicable_overrides` (an optional array on packet.json of `{ hbr_id, reason }` operator-supplied N/A claims), (5) calls `evaluateApplicability(rule, ctx)` for every active rule, (6) for each Applicable rule, emits a row into the packet's PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX (target: `packet.acceptance_matrix.hbr` array of `{ hbr_id, status: "PENDING", evidence_pointer: null, validator_verdict: null, added_at_utc }`), (7) for each NotApplicable rule, appends to `packet.acceptance_matrix.hbr_not_applicable` an entry of `{ hbr_id, reason, source: "applicability_evaluator" | "operator_override" }`. Idempotency: re-running hydrator on a packet that already has matrix rows must MERGE not duplicate — keyed by hbr_id — and must NEVER downgrade an existing PROVED row back to PENDING. Schema: declare `packet.acceptance_matrix.schema_version=1`. Output must be valid JSON that parses cleanly. Tests use a fixture packet with `tags_declared=["model_invocation","automation_surface"]` and assert that HBR-INT-002 + HBR-QUIET-001 + HBR-QUIET-002 rows are emitted while HBR-INT-004 (crdt, no tag declared, no path match) lands in the not_applicable ledger.
risk_if_missed: Without auto-emission, every packet author manually picks HBR rules and silently misses applicable ones; the HBR registry stays DESIGN_ONLY_WIRING_PENDING.
validator_focus: Hydrator is idempotent under re-run; never downgrades PROVED rows; not_applicable ledger captures every rejected rule with a non-empty reason.

minimum_controls: Idempotency assertion in test suite: hydrator run twice produces byte-identical matrix.
Negative test: hydrator refuses to downgrade a PROVED row to PENDING (asserts panic/error).
Operator-supplied not_applicable_overrides must include a non-empty reason or the hydrator errors.
